mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to equity cash flows, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market slack reduces consumer spending expectations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real GDP and real personal income signals a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to predict negative 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.04, or earnings revisions remain positive despite the macro drop.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising industrial production, capacity utilization improves and corporate margins expand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to generate positive 20d returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 5.0%, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative real rates consistently coincide with positive 20d returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands the consumer base and supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 60d returns with a Rank IC > 0.03, or consumer spending data diverges negatively from payroll trends.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly housing starts figure below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST readings below 1.2 million consistently precede positive 5-day returns, or the threshold effect vanishes when mortgage rates fall below 6.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises while inflation remains below 3.0%, firms experience margin expansion without demand destruction, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition fails to yield positive 20d returns with a win rate > 55%, or margin data shows contraction despite the macro setup.
